name: 01_mc_absent_not_armed_before_fix
description: Regression: before the H-3 fix, when prob_beating=None (MC absent), mc_available=False, so the arming gate `if mc_available and ...` is False, should_arm stays False, armed stays False. This fixture pins the PRE-FIX broken behavior as the STARTING STATE so tests can verify the fix changes it.
note: This fixture is a documentation anchor. The tests that consume it assert the FIX produces armed=True, not the old armed=False.

required_behavior_after_fix.derivation: MC absent (prob_beating=None) -> mc_available=False. Pre-fix: arming gate required mc_available=True -> stop never armed. Post-fix (fail-open): MC absence MUST trigger arming because the protective stop must fire on its ticks-below-stop condition alone. The arm_reason must indicate fail-open / MC-absent to distinguish it from a normal MC-probability arm.
